item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Imo, maybe there are notes and information in the article about 055, but we also get disinterested/unable to focus on that information, and hence don't remember reading it after finishing the article.

*insert funny joke about memory loss here*

Seriously though, it's pretty nicely written for its age. Nice work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What's darkly hilarious is that irl I keep on forgetting that this SCP exists, and am only occasionally reminded of it by something else I happen upon. In a way, everyone is effected by this SCP, because eventually we all inevitably stop thinking about it at some point…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't remember how I voted. HELP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The file describes that besides making people forget about itself, 055 also makes people feel disinterested or unable to focus when trying to describe it. Maybe they have tried to add the notes, but couldn't do it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Serious question: If it's possible to write notes, and the notes don't erase themselves, why wouldn't the descriptions in those notes be added to the file? Even if nobody ever remembers reading such descriptions, they wouldn't have any trouble actually reading it, and I don't see any reason why the information wouldn't be included.

item.title: Admin Post - Info - Closed
item.content:encoded:
Obligatory reminder that spam is not allowed, and the repeated joke about bumping old comments is very stale now.

Do not reply if you are not a staff member. This is a closed staff post.

item.title: hey
item.content:encoded: hey there

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Remember one thing… SCP-055 is not a sphere…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh that's pretty cool SCP… wait what was it again ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wouldn’t the AI become infected with the antimemetic properties too? That would render that option useless.

item.title: Re: this is going to matter
item.content:encoded: I'm on board with this: a story that takes nearly a decade to build up before they fall into place

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've upvoted this because I definitely think it's a good article. It is, in many ways, similar to the cursed SCP number only explained a lot more in depth. Something really bothers me about this article being one the top rated pages on this site though. It's good I just don't think it's THAT good. Nonetheless, I'm not going to give a downvote to an article I enjoy just because a lot of others enjoy it as well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's actually kinda smart

item.title: this is going to matter
item.content:encoded: I'm suspecting that 055 is going to be pretty critical to whatever's going on with 3125 in the rest of the antimemetics timeline. I wonder if the climax is going to tie back to it. I think the speculation in document 055-1 that SCP-055 was planted and is actively subverting the Foundation is some form or another is going to turn out correct.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have figured out a way to remeber what it is, just remember that it isn’t not that thing like this ”the objekt is not not known as SCP-055”

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since 055 only seems to delete information from human minds and not, for example, info stored in a computer, would an artificial intelligence be capable of remembering it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don’t know? That’s—.. uhh.. that’s our problem..

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea of.. well.. hmm.. what was I talkin’ about again? SGH-917 or something like that? I dunno, back to work I guess.

item.content:encoded:
Okay, calling a stop to this line of conversation since it's starting to become spammy. One ironic facetious comment about bumping old comments is more than enough; four is excessive and the joke is past stale.

Do not reply if you are not a staff member. This is a closed staff post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed, Like… HONestly: get OVER yourself!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me too! god, gets on my nerves

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is brilliant and one of the most horrifying concepts on the site. It was an interesting idea amongst the first 100 but the continued development of the idea through the years have hammered home the extreme awfulness that can be hidden behind the cognitive damage.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Brilliant idea.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yeah i hate when people bump old threads. even if they are only a couple months old.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait what am I commenting on, I forgot

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait why am i on this page again?

item.title: Great Work！
item.content:encoded:
This entry and its related Antememtics series is definitely the reason why I applied for the site- how can I not upvote that would be criminal!
This is definitely all-time classics and probably my favorite SCP article. Playing with cognition and logic and in a reverse way is just simply, not easy! And it’s clever, it’s beautiful, so LOVE it!

(I’m remembering becuz I was only talking about writing not…ur…what??

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Basically me while reading the article:

🤔

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to wonder of the possible relation of SCP 1504 and 055. Their overall effects seem somewhat similar, and perhaps 055 had some part in the acquirement of 1504's anomalous abilities. Or perhaps the two share some common origin. Though few will ponder this beyond a few minutes, I feel it's a matter worthy of deeper though.﻿

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awesome article. I am here to witness it at +1999 going onto 2000. Go 055!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It could just be an old rubber boot.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems strange that this is technically the most dangerous scp and we don’t even know what it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Even roughly 10 years after it was written, this remains one of the best works on the site, and was supremely ahead of its time. I can't wait until it reaches +2000, it deserves much more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's pretty cool that I had forgotten about this scp so I clicked to read the file again and until the moment I finished I hadn't realized it was my second time learning about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Time to wait a few more years…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea what i just upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author is qntm, an author who has written many other antimemetic articles, among other data-erasure-related things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Who's the author?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The length of time it takes to forget what 55 is is seconds but people can sometimes remember that it exists for a bit longer. As the article notes:

It appears to be possible to remember what SCP-055 is not (negations of fact), and to repeatedly deduce its existence from these memories.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Class X is actually more useful. Giving the interviewer and interviewee a class X will make it so they don't forget … The…. What were we talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait… We had a 55?? Scp-2660 sounds interesting, haven't read that skip yet

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very cool idea in general - is this the first antimemetic SCP to be posted on the site, isn't it? That's an awesome idea. However, this SCP itself doesn't seem to be internally consistent at all. Everyone forgets about it in seconds, yet an entire testing team was assembled and deployed, and an entry written about it?

Also… the "huh what was i talking about" jokes got old ten years ago.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 055 is probably the thing someone extracted from 2660 to make 2660 do what it does.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(This is from DrChandra's Updated Amnestics Guide)
Though this may not be W-Class amenstics, but:

Class W-Z Mnestics

  • Protection against anti-memetic and other mnemonic anomalies

Class W mnestics allow the subject to perceive and retain knowledge of antimemes, in addition to general memory enhancement. Class X restores awareness of previously perceived antimemes or suppressed memories. Class Y grants the subject perfect recall for any memories gained during its period of effect, and a single dose of Class Z renders the subject biochemically incapable of forgetting anything for the remainder of their lives. Class Zs are invariably fatal, with death by seizure typically resulting in a matter of hours.

So Class W/X/Y/Z mnestics can be used to possibly counter SCP-055.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you don't mind me asking, what's a Vegas Room?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If W-Class amnestics can counter anti-cognitohazards, can they counter anti-memes? Or am understanding this all wrong?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
this is when a creative author cant think of something..
..wait, why am i here?……

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe there are reasons why when you do that you also realize why it would be a bad idea. Instead you go build a Vegas Room so you can have a place where it is safe to remember this stuff so you can plan. Then you disappear and everyone who comes looking for you is also disappears.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I came up with an idea of how the Foundation can possibly remember 055. They can have someone use mnestics to remember 055 and record what they say about the SCP (assuming that 055 is antimemetic and that mnestics are canon.). I know that the effects of 055 will possibly carry over to the recording, but it's a good suggestion.

item.title: A Simple Solution
item.content:encoded: Bring The File into the chamber. Print the file and even if u forget then someone else will look at it or you'll find it when printing something else and go the the containment cell, then edit it and upload it to the database.

item.title: Re: Double negation for the win?
item.content:encoded:
This one's recent enough to reply to, right? Anyways, _I_ have a plan.

The Foundation has plenty of evidence of its existence, right? They can study it all they want affirmatively, and they'll simply forget about it. But what if we took a 682 approach to this?

Make a document outlining specific procedures for reading the document safely (as 055's effects may leak onto the rest of the document, and just in case anyways). Like "Read the table of contents carefully. Read pages ABC-XYZ only once you've read the rest of the document and are ready." Then, a list of things 055 does NOT do, or is NOT. For example, we know it's definitely not round at all. If we continued compiling negations of fact, we could deduce 055's nature.

Onto the fun part. We compile a list of things that have FAILED to destroy it. It cannot be destroyed by electric currents, as a quick example. From this list, we can deduce what we haven't tried yet, and continue the destruction efforts.

Eventually, hopefully, we destroy whatever it is, and change it from "Keter" to "Neutralized".

I think, perhaps, this file (SCP-055) could work also as a "Neutralized", in which the fact that it's already been destroyed is introduced very late in the page, though it would probably take a very, very good writer to pull it off well. Which is why I won't even try to.

Er, what was I talking about?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That would work, but no one can remember it long enough to do anything about it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Based on the description, they have a ton of recordings and the like, they just can't study them, because the effect carries over to all affirmative information about it, and recordings are inherently affirmative.

I wonder if inverting the colors or otherwise distorting a recording in known ways would help any.

item.title: Double negation for the win?
item.content:encoded: Assuming they could conceive of the plan, would double negation work? This is definitely not not one of my favorite entries I have read thus far. :-J

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Hey, we just got an order of Google Glasses."

"Why the hell did we order a bunch of Google Glasses?"

"… You know, I'm not sure."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's an antimeme (or protected by antimemes), so even if you tried to film it, which presumably the Foundation has done (and you don't need a wearable computer, just a camera), you a) would not want to film it because filming it requires thinking about it and it is an idea that you don't want to think about, which b) might result in things like "accidentally" forgetting to take off lens caps or to press record, click) would not want to save images/develop film and forget to do so and d) would just face the same problems with the images anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In order to write down orncommunicate about it meaningfully, you'd still have to process what it is, if only.for long enough to speak.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They'd loose the glasses.
